channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Latvia’s president asks opposition leader to form new government
item.description: If opposition leader Kulbergs were to form a government, the cabinet lineup would need to be approved by parliament.

item.title: Accident kills eight people in Thailand raising questions of rail safety
item.description: Devastating railway accident in Bangkok kills at least eight people. And an investigation is underway.

item.title: Francesca Albanese on Israeli sexual violence suffered by Palestinians
item.description: Francesca Albanese speaks to Redi Tlhabi about sexual violence suffered by Palestinians at the hands of Israeli forces.

item.title: Spanish Foreign Minister on Spain’s clash with the Trump administration
item.description: Jose Manuel Albares speaks to Redi Tlhabi about Spain’s commitment to international law and relations with the US.

item.title: DRC health minister warns ‘very high’ Ebola lethality rate as toll hits 80
item.description: Africa CDC concerned that outbreak of Bundibugyo strain could spread rapidly due to intense population movement.

item.title: Thousands march in central London to mark 78th Nakba anniversary
item.description: Thousands of protesters marched through central London on Saturday to mark the 78th anniversary of the Nakba

item.title: Palestinian President Abbas casts vote in decisive Fatah elections
item.description: Internal Fatah leadership elections take place amid questions of who will eventually succeed the 90-year-old leader.

item.title: UK ex-health minister says will run to replace Prime Minister Keir Starmer
item.description: Pressure mounts on Starmer's government, as Labour rivals including recently resigned Wes Streeting circle.

item.title: Al Jazeera’s exclusive report from the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Heavy ship traffic and rising regional tensions over one of the world’s most critical energy routes.

item.title: Sudanese army captures key town near Ethiopian border from RSF
item.description: Sudan’s army says it retook a vital town in southeastern Blue Nile state and inflicted heavy losses on the RSF.

item.title: Man City beat Chelsea in FA Cup final to keep treble dream alive
item.description: Premier League chasing Manchester City beat Chelsea 1-0 in tense FA Cup final at Wembley to follow up League Cup win.

item.title: Hamas confirms killing of Qassam Brigades leader in Israeli attack
item.description: Israel targeted Izz al-Din al-Haddad, the head of Hamas's armed wing in Gaza, in an attack that killed seven others.

item.title: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki: ISIL’s shadow commander in West Africa
item.description: Nigeria and the US say 'key' ISIL leader was killed in joint military operation in Borno state.

item.title: Iran warns of readiness for war and economic costs as US talks falter
item.description: Iranian leaders and TV have ramped up pressure on the US with a stream of messaging to domestic and foreign audiences.

item.title: How Thomas Massie came to represent Republican dissent in age of Trump
item.description: Massie is locked in a tight congressional primary race in Kentucky against a fellow Republican endorsed by US president.

item.title: Israeli army kills Palestinian man, raids homes in occupied West Bank
item.description: Israeli forces killed a 34-year-old man in Jenin Camp, the Palestinian health ministry said.

item.title: Tens of thousands march in London in far-right and pro-Palestine protests
item.description: British capital sees heightened security as right wing rally takes place at the same time as Nakba Day march.

item.title: Zimbabwe’s diaspora reshapes real estate and farming investment trends
item.description: Zimbabwean digital influencers appear to influence diaspora investment decisions through social media.

item.title: Mexico teachers threaten World Cup disruption over pay
item.description: Mexico’s teachers are threatening to disrupt next month’s 2026 FIFA World Cup if their demands for higher pay.

item.title: Moment of deadly train crash in Bangkok
item.description: At least eight people have been killed after a freight train crashed into a public bus in Thailand.

item.title: Ultra-Orthodox conscription dispute pushes Israeli government to brink
item.description: Israeli government faces collapse as coalition parties clash over military draft exemptions for Haredi constituents.

item.title: Amid the fertiliser crisis, Africa has a chemical-free option: Agroecology
item.description: Amid the looming fertiliser crisis, African states should invest in sustainable agriculture to increase yields and food

item.title: Robert Lewandowski to leave Barcelona with his ‘mission complete’
item.description: Veteran Polish striker helped Barca to three La Liga titles, including this season's trophy, and the 2025 Copa del Rey.

item.title: Israel’s Image Crisis: Becoming Too Big to Spin?
item.description: Israel has never spent more on its national propaganda campaigns.

item.title: Chelsea 0-1 Manchester City: FA Cup final – as it happened
item.description: All our updates, with full post-match coverage, reaction and interviews, following our text commentary stream.
